name: mansion-servants-quarters
summary: Servants' quarters — narrow beds in rows, shared trunks at the foot, a single washstand. Spartan, cold, efficient.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: rectangle, often in an attic or basement

primary_palette.note: dormitory row — four or more plain cots, trunks at the foot of each
tight quarters — bunks doubled up, a ladder each, cramped aisle
two senior servants sharing — marginally more space, a shared washstand

story_prompts: empty during the day — all staff serving upstairs, the room preserves sleeping-order
late night — a lantern moving between beds, someone sobbing quietly
a death in the household below — one bed newly empty, the trunk already being packed
a conspiracy brews — the staff meets here after midnight, a map hidden beneath a mattress
the household has dismissed its staff — beds stripped, trunks gone, a single forgotten letter

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: dim (0.2-0.3) — one or two candles, no waste of tallow

anti_patterns: NO luxury — no carpets, no paintings, no upholstery. Wool blankets only.
uniformity is the point — identical beds, identical trunks. Not personalized.
no fireplaces unless the setting demands — servants froze. That's the texture.
beds line the walls, narrow aisle between — never centered in the room
